content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a reflective rethinking of ideas and a mirrored reflection of how well understanding has been internalized, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to mimic second-language vocabulary acquisition reflexion practice: LLMs should iteratively "test" their outputs against implicit correctness benchmarks the same way language learners test their grasp of new vocabulary through repeated reflective rehearsal, rather than only contrasting inconsistent solution perspectives. This creates a low-overhead self-reflection approach that anchors abstract error correction to incremental, familiarity-based mastery.
